RelatedToFile.description:
Identifies the master file to which the GL Control is related to.  This field is used to properly isolate controls to the masters they are related to.
For example; Customer, PartClass identifies controls that are related to Customers and Part Classes

Key1.description: Major component of the foreign key of the related master record. For example: For a "Part"  control this field would contain the related Part Number,  for a "Customer"  it contains the Customer.CustNum.

Key2.description:
2nd component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent on the type of record.

Key3.description:
3rd component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent record type.

Key4.description:
4th component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent record type.

Key5.description:
5th component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent record type.

Key6.description:
6th component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent record type.

GLControlType.description: Identifier of the GL Control Type.

BusinessEntity.description: Identifies the entity.  Reference only.  Used for integrity validation when deleting a GLCTEntity record.

ExtCompanyID.description: Global Company identifier.  Used in Multi-Company Journal.

IsExternalCompany.description: Flag to indicate the account in this record is for an external company.

GlobalEntityGLC.description: Marks this EntityGLC as global, available to be sent out to other companies.

GlobalLock.description: Disables this record from receiving global updates.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

BankAcctID.description: BankAcctID of the related BankAcct record.

CallCode.description: CallCode of the related FSCallCd record.

ClassCode.description: ClassCode of the related FAClass record.

ClassID.description: ClassID.  This can be ClassID of PartClass, PRClsDed, or PRClsTax

ContractCode.description: ContractCode of the related FSContCd record.

CurrencyCode.description: CurrencyCode of the related Currency record.

CustNum.description: CustNum of the related Customer record

EmpID.description: EmpID of the related PREmpMas record.

FromPlant.description: FromPlant value of the related PlntTranDef record.

GroupCode.description: GroupCode of the related FAGroup record.

JCDept.description: JCDept of the related JCDept record.

MiscCode.description: MiscCode of the related MiscChrg or PurMisc record.

PartNum.description: PartNum of the related Part record.

Plant.description: Plant of the related PlantConfCtrl record.

ProdCode.description: ProdCode of the related ProdGrup record.

ProjectID.description: ProjectID of the related Project record.

PurchCode.description: PurchCode of the related GLPurch record.

RateCode.description: RateCode of the related GLRate record.

ReasonCode.description: ReasonCode of the related Reason record.

ReasonType.description: ReasonType of the related Reason record.

SalesCatID.description: SalesCatID of the related SalesCat record.

Shift.description: Shift value of the related JCShift record.

TaxCode.description: TaxCode of the related SalesTax record.

ToPlant.description: ToPlant value of the related PlntTranDef record.

VendorNum.description: VendorNum of the related Vendor record.

WarehouseCode.description: WarehouseCode of the related Warehse record.

PartNum.description: A unique part number that identifies this part.

SearchWord.description: An abbreviated part description field by which the user can search the Part file. In Part maintenance the Search Word is to only be updated upon initial creation of the Part with the first 8 bytes of the Part.Description.

ClassID.description:
The Inventory class that this Part belongs to. The Class field can be blank or must be valid in the PartClass master file.
Classes could be set up for different type of raw materials. It will primarily be used as a report selection parameter.

IUM.description: Primary Inventory Unit of Measure. The unit costs, are based on this uom. Used as a default for issue transactions for the part.  Part onhand and allocation quantities are tracked by this uom.  The quantities can also be tracked by other uoms (see PartUOM table) but tracking at this uom is mandatory.   Use UOMClass.DefUOMCode of the system default UOMClass  when creating new part records (see XASyst.DefUOMClassID).

PUM.description: The Purchasing Unit of measure for the Part.  During Part Maintenance the XaSyst.UM is used as a default for this field. This is used in Purchase Order entry as the default on line item details.

TypeCode.description:
Classifies Parts into the following...
M = Manufactured Part.
P = Purchased Part.
K = Sales Kit Part.B = Planning BOM.
This type code does limit referencing any part in any way. For example a type "P" can be entered on a sales order, or a type "M" can be referenced in a Purchase Order.
This field will also be used as a selection parameter in certain reports, such as Time Phase Requirements.

NonStock.description: A flag which indicates if this Part is not a stocked inventory item. This can be used so that "custom" built items which only exist per the customers order can be established as a valid part in order to provide default descriptions etc.... This can also be used for parts that are only purchased for direct use on jobs, but would normally never exist in inventory. This value will be used in report selection criteria.  It also controls the default setting of the "Make" flag in order entry line items  and the "Purchase" flag in Job material records. If a NoStock part is referenced in order entry then it defaults as "Make".  If it is referenced on a job material requirement it will default as "Purchase"

PurchasingFactor.description:
This value is used to convert quantity when there is a difference in the vendors unit of measure and how it is stocked in inventory. Example is purchased in pounds, stocked in sheets.


Formula: Issue Qty * Conversion Factor = Purchased Qty.

UnitPrice.description: Base Unit Selling Price for the Item. Maintainable only via Part Master Maintenance program. It is used as a default unit price on Sales Order line detail and on Invoice line details that are not referencing a sales order line.

PricePerCode.description: Indicates the pricing per quantity for this part. It can be "E" = per each, "C" = per hundred,  "M" = per thousand. Maintainable only via Part Maintenance. The initial default is "E". Used as default PricePerCode in order entry and invoice entry.

InternalUnitPrice.description: Base Internal Unit Selling Price for the Item.  Maintainable only via Part Master Maintenance program.  If zero, then the external unit price (Part.UnitPrice) is used.

InternalPricePerCode.description: Indicates the internal pricing per quantity for this part. It can be "E" = per each, "C" = per hundred,  "M" = per thousand.  Maintainable only via Part Maintenance.  The initial default is "E".

ProdCode.description: Product Group ID for the Part. This can be blank or must be valid in the ProdGrup file.  This will be used for report sorting and selection. Also as a default in order entry, invoice entry and job entry.

MfgComment.description: Used to enter comments for manufacturing when this part is referenced on a job. These are copied to JobHead.Comment, JobAsmbl.Comment,JobMtl.MfgComment depending on the point of reference. Commens are printed on the routing report.

PurComment.description:
Part Comments that will be used as a default for purchasing. These will be copied into the JobMtl.PurComment which then will be used to pass along to the PO when that JobMtl is referenced. It will also be copied into the PODetail.Comment field when the PO is buying the part for stock and not referencing a Job. View as an EDITOR widget.
To be view-as EDITOR widget.

CostMethod.description: Defines the Costing method to be associated with this Part. Use the XaSyst.CostMethod as a default.  When a unit cost is retrieved from the Part file the programs will use this field to determine which one of the Four sets of cost fields should be used.  A = Use Average L= Use Last S = Use Standard T = Use Avg by lot(not found in XaSyst).

UserChar1.description:
User Defined character field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserChar1Label
is non blank.

UserChar2.description:
User Defined character field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserChar2Label
is non blank.

UserChar3.description:
User Defined character field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserChar3Label
is non blank.

UserChar4.description:
User Defined character field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserChar4Label
is non blank.

UserDate1.description:
User Defined Date field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDate1Label
is non blank.

UserDate2.description: User Defined Date field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDate2Label is non blank.

UserDate3.description: User Defined Date field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDate3 Label is non blank.

UserDate4.description: User Defined Date field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDate4 Label is non blank.

UserDecimal1.description:
User Defined Decimal field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDec1Label
is non blank.

UserDecimal2.description: User Defined Decimal field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDec2Label is non blank.

UserDecimal3.description: User Defined Decimal field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDec3Label is non blank.

UserDecimal4.description: User Defined Decimal field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserDec4Label is non blank.

UserInteger1.description: User Defined Integer field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserInt1Label is non blank.

UserInteger2.description: User Defined Integer field. Actual label used is defined in the XaSyst record. This Field is only accessible if XaSyst.PartUserInt2Label is non blank.

TaxCatID.description: Indicates the Tax Category for this Part. Used as a default to Order line items or Invoice line items. Can be left blank which indicates item is taxable. If entered must be valid in the TaxCat master file.

InActive.description:
Flag which indicates if the Part Master is considered as "Inactive".
This flag will be used to exclude parts from certain searches and reports.

LowLevelCode.description: Internally assigned integer which indicates the deepest level of assembly indention that this part is used at.  This is used by the Cost Rollup routines to control the order in which parts get costed. Part at the bottom (highest levelcode) Product structure are calculated first and continues up the chain, with the final assembly parts being processed last.  This insures that when retrieving the cost of an assemblies components the components will already have had their cost rolled up.

Method.description: An internal flag which indicates that this part contains Method of Manufacture details (PartMtl/PartOpr records).  We use this to avoid processing raw material part records during processes such as BOM Cost roll up, Indented BOM lists, etc...

TrackLots.description: Indicates if Lot numbers are prompted for in transactions for this part.  Backflushing and AutoReceiving functions are ignored when TrackLots = Yes.

TrackDimension.description:
Onhand quantity is always tracked in the Parts primary inventory uom (Part.IUM). Checking this box indicates that you want to allow tracking of onhand quantity by additional uoms.
The actual UOMs to be tracked for the part are indicated by PartUOM.TrackOnHand. In order to set the PartUOM.TrackOhHand = True the Part.TrackDimension must = true.
This replaces the old 8.3 Track Dimension feature

DefaultDim.description: Default dimension code for the part.  Set by selecting a PartDim record as default.

TrackSerialNum.description: Indicates if this part is serial number tracked

CommodityCode.description: Intrastat goods classification code following the Intrastat Classification Nomenclature (ICN). The Commodity Code field can be blank to indicate the value from the part class or must be valid in the ICommCode (formerly called IStatGrp) master file.

WarrantyCode.description: Unique code for the Warranty for this part

PhantomBOM.description: A flag which indicates if this Part is a "Phantom BOM".

SalesUM.description: The Selling Unit of measure for the Part. The UOM which the unit prices are based on. Defaults as the Part.IUM.

SellingFactor.description:
This value is used to convert quantity when there is a difference in the customers unit of measure and how it is stocked in inventory. Example is sold in pounds, stocked in sheets.

Formula: Inventory Qty * Conversion Factor = Selling Qty.

MtlBurRate.description: The material burden rate for this part.

UsePartRev.description: if Yes then the part effective revision is used. If No then the revision of the demand source is used (OrderDtl, JobMtl...)

PartsPerContainer.description: Default for label printing.  Zero indicates that only one label should be produced for the entire quantity.

LotShelfLife.description: Shelf life of a lot in days.  Zero indicates unlimited shelf life.

WebPart.description: This is a Web saleable part

RunOut.description: Indicates that the onhand quantity is to be consumed and no further replenishments should be made.  Similar to Obsolete, however only warning messages will be issued to the user if they attempt new references.

SubPart.description: Indicates the default Substitute part number.  This is optional. Must be one of the related PartSub records.  This field is set indirectly when the user checks the default toggle box in Part Substitution dialog.

OnHold.description: Indicates that the part is on hold.  This feature can be used to indicate that a new part is not yet approved, that it is being phased out, has a quality issue, etc.  Further demands/supplies of this part should not be made. Similar to an "Inactive" part. However at the moment it still may have an onhand balance, supply and demands and will be reflected in stock status reporting.

OnHoldDate.description: Date that part becomes obsolete.  This can be set to a future date when the part should become obsolete.

OnHoldReasonCode.description: The Reason.Code associate with the reason why the part has been placed on hold. Valid only when Part.OnHold = Yes.

AnalysisCode.description: Default analysis code to be used when this part appears as an assembly  on a quote or a job.

GlobalPart.description: Marks the Part as a global Part, available to be sent out to other companies

GlobalLock.description: Disables this record from receiving global updates

ISSuppUnitsFactor.description: This value is used to calculate the Supplementary Units for the Intrastat.

PDMObjID.description: Holds the internal object id of pdm parts.

ImageFileName.description: Path & filename (relative to images/prod_img directory on Web Server) of .jpg product image file.

ISOrigCountry.description: This field contains the Intrastat Country of Origin Code from the Country table.

SNPrefix.description: Current setting for the prefix that will be attached to all new Serial Numbers as they are generated.

SNFormat.description: Current setting for Format of the Base serial number that will be used as new serial numbers are generated. Expressed in progress syntax. Ex: X(30), 99999999

SNBaseDataType.description: Current setting for Data type of the Base Serial Number field to be used as new serial numbers are generated. Valid values; Character, Integer

Constrained.description: Used by the scheduling process when a part is stocked.  When TRUE,  the availability of this Part must be calculated via the TimePhase process prior to scheduling a Job.

UPCCode1.description: UPS / UCC Code required by some industries.

UPCCode2.description: UPS / UCC Code required by some industries.

UPCCode3.description: UPS / UCC Code required by some industries.

EDICode.description: The Trading Partner ID that is used for incoming and outgoing EDI transactions.

WebInStock.description: For Customer Connect Only.  This field is used in Store Front to indicate if the part is available in stock.

ConsolidatedPurchasing.description: Should this Part be included in Consolidated Purchasing?

PurchasingFactorDirection.description: Indicates how Purchasing Factor is used in calculations.  If M (multiply), the Factor is multiplied, if  D (divide) the factor is divided.

SellingFactorDirection.description: Indicates how Selling Factor is used in calculations.  If M (multiply), the Factor is multiplied, if  D (divide) the factor is divided.

RecDocReq.description:
Receiving Documents Required.
Indicates receiving documents are required when receiving this part.  This pertains only to lot tracked parts that are received to inventory. If checked, then at the time of receiving the system will require that one or more attachments with a reference to a DocType having Receipt = yes be entered.Requires DocManagement license.

MDPV.description: Maximum daily production value.  Used in demand shipping schedule.

ShipDocReq.description:
Shipping Documents Required.
Indicates if shipping documents are required when shipping this part. Pertains to Inventory shipments of lot tracked parts or shipments directly from the job only. If checked, then at the time of shipping the system will require that the PartLot.Ship DocsAvail, or JobPart.ShipDocsAvail flag is true before allowing the shipment.Requires DocManagement license.

ReturnableContainer.description: The returnable container for this part when the part needs to be returned.  The value is provided by the trading partner.

QtyBearing.description: Indicates a Quantity Bearing part. Works in conjunction with the Non-Stock field to enable the part master parts to be setup for expense items.  Quantity Bearing will be set to Yes by default and only enable to be set to No if the Non-Stock flag is Yes.

NAFTAOrigCountry.description: This field contains the Country of Origin Code from the Country table.  For International shipping.

NAFTAProd.description: NAFTA Producer Code - For international shipping

UseHTSDesc.description: Use HTS description flag - for shippers shippers export declaration

RevChargeMethod.description:
Indicates what VAT Reverse Charge method needs to be applied for this Part.  The possible values are:
   "RCT"  -  "Reverse Charge with Threshold";
   "RCN"  -  "Reverse Charge with No Threshold"
Leave this field blank if no Reverse Charge should be applied in the invoice line for this Part.

RCUnderThreshold.description: Reverse Charge Under Threshold value. If the absolute value of an invoice line is less than the under threshold then the reverse charge tax code will be applied.

RCOverThreshold.description: Reverse Charge Over Threshold value. If the absolute value of an invoice line is more than the over threshold then the reverse charge tax code will be applied.

OwnershipStatus.description: This field can be used with external system integrations to identify which system currently has ownership of the record.  This field can hold either the name of the external system (example: PDM), ERP (Epicor) or null.

UOMClassID.description:
The UOM Class that will be used for the Part. The UOM Class establishes the list of unit of measures that can be used in reference to this part.
Must be valid in the UOMClass table.

SNMask.description: This is the ID by which the user will reference a particular serial number format mask.

SNMaskExample.description: BL-generated example of the serial number mask if SNBaseDataType = Mask.

SNMaskSuffix.description: A standard suffix that will be attached to all serial numbers currently used only by SNBaseStructure Mask types.

SNMaskPrefix.description: The prefix that was used to construct the serial number currently used only by SNBaseStructure Mask types.

SNLastUsedSeq.description: This is the last used serial sequence default. It is used only for the Mask Generate type to determine the next logical serial number to generate for this part/Site. It will be used when defaulting the SNLastUsedSeq field for new PartSite records.

UseMaskSeq.description: Indicates to use the value in SerialMask.SNLastUsedSeq when generating the next serial number for a Generate Mask type.

NetWeightUOM.description:
Qualifies the unit of measure of the NetWeight field.
Must be a UOMConv of the UOMClass with ClassType of "weight".   Use UOMClass.DefUOMCode of the "weight" UOMClass as a default  when creating new part records.
Having a NetWeightUOM will provides the ability to calculate total  weight.

NetVolumeUOM.description:
Qualifies the unit of measure of the NewVolume field.
Must be a UOMConv of the UOMClass with ClassType of "volume".   Use UOMClass.DefUOMCode of the "volume" UOMClass as a default  when creating new part records.
Having a Net Volume UOM will provides the ability to calculate total volume

LotBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotMfgBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotMfgLot.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG Lot is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotHeat.description: Indicates if entry of a Heat Number  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotFirmware.description: Indicates if entry of FIRMWARE is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotBeforeDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Best Before Date is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotMfgDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Original Manufacture Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotCureDt.description: Indicates if entry of a CURE DATE  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotExpDt.description: Indicates if entry of an Expiration Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotPrefix.description: Defines a prefix to be used when a lot number is generated for the specific part.

LotUseGlobalSeq.description: When generating the numeric portion of a lot number it can be either based on a next available number for the part (see Part.LotNextNum) or next available number from a Global Sequence (see LotSeq table and Part.LotSeqID)

LotSeqID.description: The LotSeqID of the LotSeq record to use to retreive next available number when the part is using a Global Sequence  (Part.LotUseGlobalSeq = True). Must be valid in the LotSeq table if Part.LotUseGlobalSeq = True)

LotNxtNum.description: The next available number to use to generate new lot numbers a part when the  is configured to use "Part Specific" number sequence. (Part.LotUseGlobalSeq = false).

LotDigits.description: Number of digits of the Next Avail Lot Number controls that will be used by system Generate lot number logic.

LotLeadingZeros.description: If leading zeros should be included in the numeric portion of the system generated lot number.

LotAppendDate.description:
Option to append a trailing date string to the system generated lot number. The Date is the current system date.
Valid options are: None (Default), DD, MM, YYYY, MMYYYY, MM_YYYY, DDMMYYY, DD-MM-YYY, MMDDYYYY, MM-DD-YYYY,  YYYYMMDD, YYYY-MM-DD

BuyToOrder.description: This flag identifies those parts that will suggest a PO each time than a sales order is created. This flag will be used as a default in the sales order.

DropShip.description: This flag identifies those parts that are commonly drop shipped. This flag will be used as a default in the sales order.

RefCategory.description: The reference category that this Part belongs to.

CSFCJ5.description:
Malaysia Localization
The flag to indicate that the part is under CJ5 jurisdiction

CSFLMW.description:
Malaysa Localization
The flag to indicate that the part is under LMW jurisdiction

GrossWeightUOM.description:
Qualifies the unit of measure of the GrossWeight field.
Must be a UOMConv of the UOMClass with ClassType of "weight".   Use UOMClass.DefUOMCode of the "weight" UOMClass as a defaul
t  when creating new part records.

BasePartNum.description: The part number used to identify the configured part number this part number was generated from.

FSPricePerCode.description: Indicates the field service sales pricing per quantity for this part. It can be "E" = per each, "C" = per hundred,  "M" = per thousand. The initial default is "E".

RcvInspectionReq.description: Indicates if  Inspection is required upon receipt.  Inspection will also be enforced if the related Part Class, Vendor, PO Detail, JobMtl or JobOper have their "RcvInspectionReq" fields set to Yes.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PartSpecificPackingUOM.description: Indicates if the packaging information is part specific or specified at the UOM class level.

CNSpecification.description: Specification Code for China GTI purposes

SyncToExternalCRM.description: This field defines if the part  is synchronized to an External CRM.

ExternalCRMPartID.description: This field holds the id of this part in the External CRM

ExternalCRMLastSync.description: This field defines the last time that the  part  has been Synchronized between Epicor ERP and the External CRM. This field is maintained by the External CRM Synchronization  process.

ExternalCRMSyncRequired.description: This fields determines if the part needs to be synchronized to the External CRM. If there are changes in the part master file , Epicor ERP automatically turns on this field.

CNCodeVersion.description: Code Version for China GTI purposes

CNTaxCategoryCode.description: Tax Category Code for China GTI purposes

CNHasPreferentialTreatment.description: Has Preferential Treatment value for China GTI purposes

CNPreferentialTreatmentContent.description: Preferential Treatment Content for China GTI purposes

CNZeroTaxRateMark.description: Zero Tax Rate Mark for China GTI purposes

CreatedOn.description: User the Part was created by

AttBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttMfgBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttMfgLot.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG Lot is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttHeat.description: Indicates if entry of a Heat Number  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttFirmware.description: Indicates if entry of FIRMWARE is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttBeforeDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Best Before Date is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttMfgDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Original Manufacture Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttCureDt.description: Indicates if entry of a CURE DATE  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttExpDt.description: Indicates if entry of an Expiration Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

DeferPurchaseReceipt.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later.  This flag is used for following screens: Container Receipt, Receipt Entry.

DeferJobReceipt.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later. This flag is used for following screens: Job Receipt to Job, Job Receipt to Salvage, Job Receipt to Inventory, Kanban Receipts.

DeferInspection.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later.  This flag is used for following screens: Inspection Processing.

DeferQtyAdjustment.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later.  This flag is used for following screens: Quantity Adjustment.

DeferInventoryMove.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later. This flag is used for following screens: Inventory Transfer.

DeferShipments.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later. This flag is used for following screens: Customer Shipment Entry, Subcontractor Shipment Entry, Drop Shipment Entry, Order Entry.

DeferInventoryCounts.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later. This flag is used for following screens: Count Tag Entry.

DeferReturnMaterials.description: This flag indicates if the Lots attributes are necessary to be entered in the creation of the lot or could be entered later.  This flag is used for following screens: RMA Processing.

ChangedOn.description: Date/Time when the Part record was updated

SendToFSA.description: Determines if the Part has to be synchronized with Epicor FSA application.

ExternalMESSyncRequired.description: This field determines if the record needs to be synchronized to the Machine MES. Changes to the record will automatically set the value to true.

ExternalMESLastSync.description: The date and time the record was last synched to Machine MES.  The field is maintained by the Export Mattec process.

FSAItem.description: When the part is marked as Item, it will create an Item Resource in Epicor FSA.

FSAEquipment.description: When the part is marked as Equipment, it will create an Equipment Resource Template in Epicor FSA.

BOLClass.description: Bill of Lading Class. Additional data for the part required for LTL and International shipments.

FairMarketValue.description: Fair Market Value. Additional data for the part required for LTL and International shipments.

LocationIDNumReq.description: Indicates if this part requires Identification Numbers shipment time.  This is disable if Track Location inventory is false.

LocationTrackInv.description: Indicates if this part tracks Location Inventory.

LocationMtlView.description: Set the default value of Location View for materials added in Engineering Workbench.

LocationFormatID.description: Default format ID used when assigning ID Numbers.

TrackInventoryAttributes.description: Indicates if inventory for this part is tracked at the attribute level. This feature requires the Advanced Unit of Measure license.

DefaultAttributeSetID.description: The unique identifier of the related Dynamic Attribute Set.

AttISOrigCountry.description: Indicates if entry of a County of Origin is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

TrackInventoryByRevision.description: Indicates if inventory for this part is tracked by revision number.

PlanningByRevision.description: Indicates if this part performs MRP by Revision.  Requires Planning by Revision license.

Saleable.description: Indicates if Part is saleable, non-saleable or non-saleable with warning.

CarbonMtlBurRate.description: The carbon material burden rate for this part.

PartsPerPackage.description: Default for shipping package. Indicates how may parts from the shipping line will fit in a case for shipping. Zero indicates that only one case will be created for the shipping line.

CasePkgCode.description: Package Code default for shipping package.

EnableExpressCheckOut.description: Should the Express Part Check Out option be enabled?

EnableInActive.description: Indicates if the InActive flag should be available for input,

EnableIUM.description: Flag to tell UI whether the Part.IUM field should be enabled or not.

EnableRevCharge.description: Indicates if Override Reverse Charge check box should be enabled.

EnableSerialNum.description: Indicates if the Serial Number button should be enabled.

EnableTrackSerialNum.description: This field is used only as a flag to determine in UI, if the Part.TrackSerialNum can be change.

EnableUOMClass.description: Flag to tell UI whether the UOMClassID field should be enabled or not.

FSAInstallationCost.description: Default installation price of an equipment that requires installation in Epicor FSA.

FSAInstallationRequired.description: Indicates if the equipment requires an installation prior being marked as “Installed” on a Location in Epicor FSA. If true, at shipment it will create a service order for the installation service in FSA.

FSAInstallationType.description: Indicates the service order template ID that Epicor FSA will use to create the installation service order.

GlbFlag.description: Indicates if the Part is Global (master or linked)

GlbLink.description: Delimited list of GlbCompany and GlbPartNum that is linking to this part

GlbTableAllowUpdTrackDim.description: check if TrackDimension is in GlbTable and should be disabled in Part Entry

GlbTableAllowUpdTrackLots.description: check if TrackLots is in GlbTable and should be disabled in Part Entry

GlbTableAllowUpdTrackSerial.description: check if TrackSerialNum is in GlbTable and should be disabled in Part Entry

HasOnHandQty.description: Indicates if there is any quantity on hand for this part

IsComponent.description: Indicates if part is a component (has a where used list available)

IsCoPart.description:
This field indicates if the part is being used as a co-part anywhere.  This field will be used to prevent a Part from being marked as serial tracked or configured after being added as a co-part.

CoParts Project.

ISOrigCountryNum.description: This is the numeric value of ISOrigCountry.

NextGeneratedLotNum.description: Shows what the next generated lot number for this part would look like

PLMEnabled.description: Indicates if the PLM toggle box is enabled.

UpdatePartPlant.description: Yes means to copy the NonStock and CostMethod from Part to all the PartPlant records.

UpdateSNPartPlant.description: Indicates whether to update the Part serial number format changes to part plant

COASegReferences.description: List of fields which are referenced by COA segments.

HasMRPPlanningAttribute.description: If this Part is TrackInventoryAttributes = true, and the AttrClassID it is associated to has one or more attributes whose DynAttrClassDtl.UsedInPlanning= true.

EnableTrackByRevision.description: This field is used only as a flag to determine in UI, if the Part.TrackInventoryByRevision can be changed.

LinkedToGlbPart.description: indicated if this part has been linked to a global part

PartNum.description: The PartNum of the Part master to which this record is related.

RevisionNum.description: Revision number which is used to uniquely identify the revision of the part.

ChangeDate.description: System Date when this change was made.

ChangeTime.description: System Time (seconds since midnight) of when the changes were made.

ChangedBy.description: UserID who made the changes.  Not maintainable by the user.

ChangeDescription.description: Used to enter a description of the changes that were made.

AltMethod.description: Alternate Routing method to be used for this revision.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

ProcessMfgID.description: The identifier of related Process Manufacturing.

DspChangeTime.description: Display ChangeTime in HH:MM AM format

PartNum.description: Identifies the Part Number. It must be valid in the Part table.

WarehouseCode.description: Contains the Warehouse code of where this part exists. This must be valid in the WareHouse table.

BinNum.description: Identifies the Bin location that contains an Onhand quantity for this Part within a warehouse. The PartBin.BinNum can be blank (indicating bin tracking is not used for this part) or it  must be valid in the WhseBin table.

MinimumQty.description: Indicates the desired minimum on-hand quantity.

MaximumQty.description: Use to set a Maximum quantity limit that is desired to be on-hand.

SafetyQty.description: Safety quantity is a "purchasing cushion" limit.

KBPONUM.description: Purchase order number  that the detail line item is linked to.

KBPOLine.description: The line number of the detail record on the purchase order.  This number uniquely identifies the record within the Purchase Order number.  The number not directly maintainable, it's assigned by the system when records are created. The user references this item during PO receipt process.

KBBinNum.description: Identifies the Bin location that contains an Onhand quantity for this Part within a warehouse. The PartBin.BinNum can be blank (indicating bin tracking is not used for this part) or it  must be valid in the WhseBin table.

KBPlant.description: Site Identifier. This field cannot be blank.

OnhandQty.description: Holds the Quantity onhand for this Part in the warehouse in the specific bin location.  Whenever this quantity becomes zero the record should be deleted. This quantity needs to added to or subtracted from Manufactured receipts, Purchased receipts, Physical inventories, Inventory issues, Warehouse Transfers, Shipping and Adjustments.

KBQty.description: Indicates the desired minimum on-hand Kanban quantity.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

AttributeSetID.description: The unique identifier of the related Dynamic Attribute Set.

Plant.description: Filled in by BO, not phiscally in database.

PartNum.description: A unique part number that identifies this part.

QtyPerc.description: Qty percent of this part which is from this country of origin.

ValuePerc.description: Value percent of this part from this country of origin.

Primary.description: Is this the primary country of origin for this part

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PartNum.description: The PartNum field identifies the Part and is used as part of the primary key.

RevisionNum.description: Revision number which is used to uniquely identify the revision of the part, and is used as part of the primary key

CoPartNum.description: Companion PartNum identifies the Part that is manufactured along with the main part (ex: Right and Left parts)

CoRevisionNum.description: Revision number which is used to uniquely identify the revision of the companion part, and is used as part of the primary key

PartsPerOp.description:
Part Per Operation. Active only for Concurrent process
Jobs. Otherwise set to 1.

LbrCostBase.description:
Defines an integer value which is used to calculate a
ratio for prorating the labor costs to the end part. For example a job produces parts A and B, and you want part B to have cost 2 times that of the cost of Part A.  Part A CostBase would be 1 and B would be 2.

MtlCostBase.description:
Defines an integer value which is used to calculate a
ratio for prorating the material costs to the end part. For example a job produces parts A and B, and you want part B to have cost 2 times that of the cost of Part A.  Part A CostBase would be 1 and B would be 2.

IUM.description: Defines the Unit of Measure used when part is issued, this is also how it is stocked.  Use the value from XaSyst.UM as a default when creating new part records.

AltMethod.description: Alternate Routing method to be used for this revision, and is used as part of the primary key

PreventSugg.description: If true, MRP will not generate change suggestions for the co-part

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PrimaryCost.description: Indicates if the parent Part should be used as the primary costing method for the co-part

ProcessMfgID.description: The identifier of related Process Manufacturing.

PartNum.description: The PartNum field identifies the Part.

DimCode.description: Unique dimension code for the part.

DUM.description: Dimension unit of measure. Cannot be blank. Defaults to part's unit of measure.

DimConvFactor.description:
Dimension conversion factor.  This conversion factor is used to convert the qty to the base part unit of measure.
Example: A half sheet to full sheet conversion factor would be 2 and a double sheet to full sheet conversion factor would be 0.5.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PartNum.description: The PartNum field identifies the Part and is used as the primary key.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PartNum.description: A unique part number that identifies this part.

SearchWord.description: An abbreviated part description field by which the user can search the Part file. In Part maintenance the Search Word is to only be updated upon initial creation of the Part with the first 8 bytes of the Part.Description.

ClassID.description:
The Inventory class that this Part belongs to. The Class field can be blank or must be valid in the PartClass master file.
Classes could be set up for different type of raw materials. It will primarily be used as a report selection parameter.

IUM.description: Primary Inventory Unit of Measure. The unit costs, are based on this uom. Used as a default for issue transactions for the part.  Part onhand and allocation quantities are tracked by this uom.  The quantities can also be tracked by other uoms (see PartUOM table) but tracking at this uom is mandatory.   Use UOMClass.DefUOMCode of the system default UOMClass  when creating new part records (see XASyst.DefUOMClassID).

PUM.description: The Purchasing Unit of measure for the Part.  During Part Maintenance the XaSyst.UM is used as a default for this field. This is used in Purchase Order entry as the default on line item details.

TypeCode.description:
Classifies Parts into the following...
M = Manufactured Part.
P = Purchased Part.
K = Sales Kit Part.B = Planning BOM.
This type code does limit referencing any part in any way. For example a type "P" can be entered on a sales order, or a type "M" can be referenced in a Purchase Order.
This field will also be used as a selection parameter in certain reports, such as Time Phase Requirements.

NonStock.description: A flag which indicates if this Part is not a stocked inventory item. This can be used so that "custom" built items which only exist per the customers order can be established as a valid part in order to provide default descriptions etc.... This can also be used for parts that are only purchased for direct use on jobs, but would normally never exist in inventory. This value will be used in report selection criteria.  It also controls the default setting of the "Make" flag in order entry line items  and the "Purchase" flag in Job material records. If a NoStock part is referenced in order entry then it defaults as "Make".  If it is referenced on a job material requirement it will default as "Purchase"

ProdCode.description: Product Group ID for the Part. This can be blank or must be valid in the ProdGrup file.  This will be used for report sorting and selection. Also as a default in order entry, invoice entry and job entry.

InActive.description:
Flag which indicates if the Part Master is considered as "Inactive".
This flag will be used to exclude parts from certain searches and reports.

Method.description: An internal flag which indicates that this part contains Method of Manufacture details (PartMtl/PartOpr records).  We use this to avoid processing raw material part records during processes such as BOM Cost roll up, Indented BOM lists, etc...

TrackLots.description: Indicates if Lot numbers are prompted for in transactions for this part.  Backflushing and AutoReceiving functions are ignored when TrackLots = Yes.

TrackDimension.description:
Onhand quantity is always tracked in the Parts primary inventory uom (Part.IUM). Checking this box indicates that you want to allow tracking of onhand quantity by additional uoms.
The actual UOMs to be tracked for the part are indicated by PartUOM.TrackOnHand. In order to set the PartUOM.TrackOhHand = True the Part.TrackDimension must = true.
This replaces the old 8.3 Track Dimension feature

TrackSerialNum.description: Indicates if this part is serial number tracked

PhantomBOM.description: A flag which indicates if this Part is a "Phantom BOM".

OnHold.description: Indicates that the part is on hold.  This feature can be used to indicate that a new part is not yet approved, that it is being phased out, has a quality issue, etc.  Further demands/supplies of this part should not be made. Similar to an "Inactive" part. However at the moment it still may have an onhand balance, supply and demands and will be reflected in stock status reporting.

QtyBearing.description: Indicates a Quantity Bearing part. Works in conjunction with the Non-Stock field to enable the part master parts to be setup for expense items.  Quantity Bearing will be set to Yes by default and only enable to be set to No if the Non-Stock flag is Yes.

LotBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotMfgBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotMfgLot.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG Lot is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotHeat.description: Indicates if entry of a Heat Number  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotFirmware.description: Indicates if entry of FIRMWARE is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotBeforeDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Best Before Date is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotMfgDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Original Manufacture Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotCureDt.description: Indicates if entry of a CURE DATE  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

LotExpDt.description: Indicates if entry of an Expiration Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

AttBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttMfgBatch.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG BATCH is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttMfgLot.description: Indicates if entry of a MFG Lot is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttHeat.description: Indicates if entry of a Heat Number  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttFirmware.description: Indicates if entry of FIRMWARE is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttBeforeDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Best Before Date is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttMfgDt.description: Indicates if entry of a Original Manufacture Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttCureDt.description: Indicates if entry of a CURE DATE  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

AttExpDt.description: Indicates if entry of an Expiration Date  is required for Lots of this Part. Pertinent only for lot tracked parts. Accepted values are N="Not Tracked", T = "Tracked" and M = "Mandatory".

TrackInventoryAttributes.description: Indicates if inventory for this part is tracked at the attribute level. This feature requires the Advanced Unit of Measure license.

DefaultAttributeSetID.description: The unique identifier of the related Dynamic Attribute Set.

HasMRPPlanningAttribute.description: If this Part is TrackInventoryAttributes = true, and the AttrClassID it is associated to has one or more attributes whose DynAttrClassDtl.UsedInPlanning= true.

TrackInventoryByRevision.description: Indicates if inventory for this part is tracked by revision number.

RevisionNum.description: Optional field that contains the customers revision. Default from the PartRev.RevisionNum field.

PartNum.description: A unique part number that identifies this part.

PCType.description:
Identifies the type of product code. This value can be used to find a Product code for a specific PartNum/ Type/UOM combination.
Currentlt the following types are valid;
"UPC-12,EAN-13,EAN-14,EAN-8,GTIN-14" 
This is for reference only and performs not other function.

UOMCode.description: Unit of Measure code that will be defaulted in when this Product Code is used. Must be a Unit of measure that is valid within the Parts UOMClass.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PartNum.description: Part Number.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

Plant.description: Site Identifier. This field cannot be blank.

PartNum.description: The PartNum field identifies the Part and is used in the primary key.

PrimWhse.description: Defines which warehouse is to be used as the Primary Warehouse for this part in this Site. A primary warehouse is the one that this part is most commonly found in.  This warehouse is used as the default in many programs, such as entry of sales order line

MinimumQty.description: Indicates the desired minimum on-hand quantity. This is used by the time phase requirements report when user requests to show any parts that will or have fallen below this level. It is also used as a selection parameter for the inventory reorder report. This is an optional field.

MaximumQty.description: Use to set a Maximum quantity limit that is desired to be on-hand. This field is used as a selection option by the inventory reorder report to show all parts that are over this limit. This field is optional.

SafetyQty.description:
Safety quantity is a "purchasing cushion" limit. It's the amount you would need to have to cover your requirements until a shipment arrives from the vendor. If your on-hand quantity falls below this limit it means that there is a good chance that you will run out of material before the next shipment arrives. This value is used by the inventory reorder report and the time phase report. It is an optional field.
Note: Safety + Minimum = Reorder Point...  using this formula the  reorder point is the amount at which to reorder to maintain at least the prescribed minimum quantity.

MinOrderQty.description: Used to establish a suggested Order Qty when purchasing this Part for this Site. This value will be shown on the time phase report.

LeadTime.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

VendorNum.description: Number of Vendor master that this part is normally purchased from. The Purchase Order Management module uses it.  used in suggested vendor analysis.

BackFlush.description: Indicates if this part should be backflushed for this Site. Backflushing is the process of automatically issuing the material to jobs based on the operation quantity completed.  When completed and scrap quantities are reported to a job operation (via labo

MfgLotSize.description: This is the lot size that is used when performing a BOM cost rollup to distribute setup costs.

MinMfgLotSize.description: This is the minimum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is less than this amount then MRP will create a job with this production quantity.  The excess amount will be sent to stock.  Zero is no minimum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.

MaxMfgLotSize.description: This is the maximum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is greater than this amount then MRP will create additional job(s) to satisfy the required production quantity.  Zero is no maximum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.  Example:  Required Quantity = 500, Maximum Lot Size = 150, 4 jobs will be created with production quantities of 150, 150, 150, and 50.

MfgLotMultiple.description: This is the manufacturing lot size multiple.  MRP will create jobs in multiples of this field.  Any excess amount will be sent to stock.  Zero is no lot multiple (lot-for-lot).  Example:  Required Quantity = 500, Lot Multiple = 150, Lot Maximum = 450, 2 jobs will be created with production quantities of 450, and 150.

DaysOfSupply.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

ReOrderLevel.description: This is the flag indicating the inventory level we need to bring up to when it falls below re-order point(safety + minimum). Valid values are MAX and MIN.

MRPRecalcNeeded.description: System maintained field.  Indicates that MRP needs to be recalculated for this part/Site.

ProcessMRP.description: Flag indicating if MRP should process this part.

GenerateSugg.description: Flag indicating if PO suggestion should be generated for this part.

GetFromLocalWhse.description: This flag controls if a supply is always created in this Site for a part. If it is no then the default Site that provides a supply is from a part's product group unless the Site from product group is blank, in that case the default Site should be from the

ForecastTime.description: Number of days forward to capture SugPODtl records for transfer as Forecast records.  Only for Intercompany trading partners

TransferPlant.description: Default Site that part is transfered from when it is obtained via Site transfer.

SourceType.description:
Indicates the normal source for this part in the Site.
Values are; K = Sales Kit,M = Manufactured,P = Purchased,T = Transferred.B = Planning BOM. 
Initial default is base on Part.TypeCode.

TransferLeadTime.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part from the transfer Site to this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional.

PrepTime.description: Used to determine the start date

ReceiveTime.description: Days needed to move part to stock or next job.  Deducted from Due Date.

PlanTimeFence.description: Days out from the current date when dates on jobs, PO, TO cannot be changed

ReschedOutDelta.description: MRP parameter not to reschedule if number of days change below

ReschedInDelta.description: Same as ReschedOutDelta but for messages

NonStock.description: A flag which indicates if this Part is not a stocked inventory item. This can be used so that "custom" built items which only exist per the customers order can be established as a valid part in order to provide default descriptions etc.... This can also be used for parts that are only purchased for direct use on jobs, but would normally never exist in inventory. This value will be used in report selection criteria.  It also controls the default setting of the "Make" flag in order entry line items  and the "Purchase" flag in Job material records. If a NoStock part is referenced in order entry then it defaults as "Make".  If it is referenced on a job material requirement it will default as "Purchase"

PhantomBOM.description: A flag which indicates if this Part is a "Phantom BOM".

BuyerID.description: Identifies the Buyer for the part class. Used as the default in the Automated Purchasing process.

PersonID.description:
Identifies the Person to be used as the default Production Planner. This person is responsible for handling  the manufacturing suggestions of parts in this Product Group.
Used as the default for "new" manufacturing suggestions and on Job Header. Manufacturing suggestions can be filtered by Planner.
Relates to the Person table.

CostMethod.description: Defines the Costing method to be associated with this Part. Use the XaSyst.CostMethod as a default.  When a unit cost is retrieved from the Part file the programs will use this field to determine which one of the Four sets of cost fields should be used.  A = Use Average L= Use Last S = Use Standard T = Use Avg by lot(not found in XaSyst).

AltMethod.description: Alternate Routing method to be used for this Part/Site

KitTime.description: For Manufactured Parts to determine the Due date of the material

KitAllowUpdate.description: Indicates if kit component lines can be added, deleted and modified during Sales Order and Quote entry.

KitShipComplete.description: Indicates if the parent kit part must be shipped complete or if kit components can be shipped in varying degrees of completeness.  If this field is set to "No", then KitPricing must be set to "P" .

KitAllowChangeParms.description: Indicates if changes the kit parameters is allowed during Sales Order and Quote entry.

KitBackFlush.description: Indicates if all components are to be backflushed when a kit parent item is shipped.

KitPricing.description: Indicates how kits will be priced.  Values are: P = Kit Parent Pricing (The price is obtained from the sales price for the kit parent item), C = Component Pricing (The price is obtained from a rollup of kit component items).

KitPrintCompsPS.description: Indicates if kit components are to be printed on packing slips.  If KitShipComplete is "Yes", then this field must be set to "Yes" as well.

KitPrintCompsInv.description: Indicates if kit components are to be printed on invoices.  If KitShipComplete is "Yes", then this field must be set to "Yes" as well.

ShortHorizonDays.description: Number of days out that the ShortHorizonMinMfgLotSize and ShortHorizonMaxMfgLotSize will be used instead of MinLotSize and MaxLotSize.

ShortHorizonMinMfgLotSize.description: This is the Short Horizon minimum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is less than this amount then MRP will create a job with this production quantity.  The excess amount will be sent to stock.  Zero is no minimum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.

ShortHorizonMaxMfgLotSize.description: This is the Short Horizon maximum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is greater than this amount then MRP will create additional job(s) to satisfy the required production quantity.  Zero is no maximum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.  Example:  Required Quantity = 500, Maximum Lot Size = 150, 4 jobs will be created with production quantities of 150, 150, 150, and 50.

LimitProdYldRecalc.description: If set = true and the production yield is being recalculated for an assembly or any of its subassemblies, then the recalculation and quantity adjustments will stop at the assembly level and reduce the overrun quantity and if necessary adjust the PullQty rather than rolling up to its parent

QtyBearing.description: Sets the default for Part.QtyBearing. The Part.QtyBearing fields works in conjunction with the Part.Non-Stock field to enable the part master parts to be setup for expense items.

MRPLastRunDate.description: System date on which the last MRP processing was run.

MRPLastRunTime.description: System Time (hr-min-sec) when the last MRP process was run.

MRPLastScheduledDate.description: Scheduled Date used in last MRP run

MRPLastCutOffDate.description: Cut Off Date used in last MRP run

ShortHorizonDaysSupp.description: Used to record the short horizon order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

SNMask.description: If the SNBaseDataType is Mask this is the Serial Mask ID assigned for format validation/generation.

SNMaskExample.description: BL-generated example of the serial number mask if SNBaseDataType = Mask.

SNBaseDataType.description:
Current setting for Data type of the Base Serial Number field to be used as new serial numbers are generated. Valid values; Character, Integer, Mask
Code/desc required:
CHARACTER`Alphanumeric
INTEGER`NumericOnly
MASK`Serial Mask
This field should be flagged as Include = true in Object Designer.

SNFormat.description:
Current setting for Format of the Base serial number that will be used as new serial numbers are generated. Expressed in progress syntax. Ex: X(30), 99999999 for Character or Integer, or as a Serial Mask defined in SerialMask table.
This field should be flagged as ReadOnly and Include = true in Object Designer.

SNPrefix.description:
Current setting for the prefix that will be attached to all new Serial Numbers as they are generated for Character and Integer format types.
This field should be flagged as Include = true in Object Designer.

SNMaskSuffix.description: A standard suffix that will be attached to all serial numbers generated for the PartSite currently used only by SNBaseStructure Mask types

SNMaskPrefix.description: The prefix that was used to construct the serial number currently used only by SNBaseStructure Mask types

SNLastUsedSeq.description: This is the last used serial sequence. It is used only for the Mask Generate type to determine the next logical serial number to generate for this part/Site. It can be altered by the user and several PartSites can have the same counter values defined, but

UseMaskSeq.description: Indicates to use the value in SerialMask.SNLastUsedSeq when generating the next serial number for a Generate Mask type.

BuyToOrder.description: This flag identifies those parts that will suggest a PO each time than a sales order is created. This flag will be used as a default in the sales order.

DropShip.description: This flag identifies those parts that are commonly drop shipped. This flag will be used as a default in the sales order.

PcntTolerance.description: Percent Tolerance. Entered as a positive number, Used to define the default percent tolerance for parts for which no percent tolerance is set up in PartWhse. Zero indicates that any quantity variance is considered out of tolerance. A number greater than zero indicates that a percentage difference between the count quantity and the frozen quantity by more than the percent tolerance figure is considered out of tolerance. Calculated as ?adjustment qty / frozen qty? expressed as a percent.

CalcPcnt.description: False = there is no percent tolerance consideration and any percent variance is considered within tolerance. True = percent tolerance is active for this part/Site and the value in PcntTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance is within tol

CalcQty.description: False = there is no quantity tolerance consideration and any quantity variance is considered within tolerance. True = quantity tolerance is active for this part/Site and the value in QtyTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance is within t

CalcValue.description: False = there is no value tolerance consideration and any value variance is considered within tolerance. True = value tolerance is active for this part/Site and the value in ValueTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance is within toleranc

QtyAdjTolerance.description: Used to provide a means to control whether a count quantity discrepancy should be posted as an adjustment to inventory. This value is used for parts for which no qty adj tolerance is set up in PartWhse. Zero indicates all quantity adjustments will be posted. This parameter is used to control the count discrepancy of parts that are counted by weight on a scale. Counts often vary based upon humidity. If the count of the part is within this tolerance but different from the frozen quantity then no inventory adjustment will be posted.

QtyTolerance.description: Quantity tolerance. Entered as a positive whole number, Used to define the default quantity tolerance for parts for which no quantity tolerance is set up in PartWhse. Zero indicates that any quantity variance is considered out of tolerance. A number greater than zero indicates that a count quantity that varies from the frozen quantity by more than the quantity tolerance figure is considered out of tolerance.

ValueTolerance.description: Value tolerance. Entered as a positive number. Used to define the default value tolerance for parts for which no value tolerance is set up in PartWhse, PartSite, WarehseABC or SiteConfABC  An entry of zero will indicate that any value variance will be con

DemandQty.description: This is a summary of the total outstanding manufacturing allocation requirements for this Part in this Site.  It is updated from Order Release records that are being manufactured. The outstanding  Sales Order Requirements is calculated from Open ORderRel

ReservedQty.description: This is a summary of the total outstanding manufacturing requirements for this Part in this Site.  It is updated from Order Release records that are being manufactured. The outstanding  Sales Order Requirements is calculated from Open ORderRel records as

AllocatedQty.description: This is a summary of the total outstanding job allocation requirements for this Part in this Site.

DeleteMRPJobs.description: Flag indicates the need to delete unfirm Jobs even if MRP is run with the recycle job option

TotMfgLeadTimeSys.description: System calculated manufacturing lead time.  This is the total lead time needed to generate the part, which includes the time on lower level parts, lead times, etc.  Not editable by the user.

TotMfgLeadTimeMnl.description: Manually entered manufacturing lead time.  This is the total lead time needed to generate the part.  Directly maintained by the user.

LvlMfgLeadTimeSys.description: Displays the manufacturing lead time calculated by the system. This is the lead time required to manufacture the part at the level of this part, it includes the time to manufacture the subassemblies in the same job (Pull as Assembly materials). The user cannot edit this value.

LvlMfgLeadTimeMnl.description: Displays the manufacturing lead time calculated by the system. This is the lead time required to manufacture the part at the level of this part, it includes the time to manufacture the subassemblies in the same job (Pull as Assembly materials). Directly maintained by the user.

MfgLeadTimeCalcDate.description: The date the system manufacturing lead times (TotMfglLeadTimeSys and LvlMfgLeadTimeSys) were calculated.

MfgLeadTimeMnlDate.description: The date the manual manufacturing lead times (TotMfgLeadTimeSys and LvlMfgLeadTimeSys) were entered by the user.

AutoConsumeStock.description: Auto consume available stock when MRP runs and creates a job.

StartMinLotQty.description: Start the minimum lot quantity for a job when there is enough quantity to do so.  If enough quantity is available for the minimum lot quantity, the job will be split - one job for the quantity that can be started, another job for the remaining quantity.  Used when MRP creates jobs.

MinLotLeadTime.description: The lead time to consider for constrained materials when determining if a quantity can be started on a job.  Applicable when StartMinLotQty is true.

MfgLeadTimeMnl.description: Indicates manufacturing lead times are entered manually by the user.

MfgLeadTimeEnteredBy.description: Userid of user who entered manual manufacturing lead times.

MinStartQty.description: Indicates the minumum quantity that can be started when splitting a job.  Used when the StartMinLotQty option is selected.

MultiLevelCTP.description: Available for stock manufactured parts.  Indicates if capable to promise considers sub-assemblies when determining the capable to promise date.  When false, capable to promise only looks at ATP for the capable to promise part - subassemblies are not considered.

RcvInspectionReq.description: Indicates if  Inspection is required for items received from this vendor.  Inspection will also be enforced if the related PartClass, Vendor, Podetail, JobMtl or JobOper have their "RcvInspectionReq" fields set to Yes.

ConsumeSafety.description: Indicates if MRP should allow consumption of safety stock within the purchase lead time

SLTVendorNum.description: Number of Alternate Vendor master that this part can be purchased from with short lead times. The Purchase Order will be generated for this supplier when suggestions fall within the purchasing lead time and the projected supply drops below safely.

ShortLeadTime.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

ExternalMES.description: Indicates the record is used with Machine MES

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

UrgentLeadTime.description: This is the lead time used when generating a new suggestion within the lead time window.  If this field is 0 and the Supplier is determined from the Supplier Price List, the suggestion will use the lead time from the price list.

UrgentMinOrdQty.description: This is the minimum qty required when generating a new suggestion within the lead time window.

UrgentMultQty.description: This is used to calculate the suggestion qty to the nearest multiple when generating a new suggestion within the lead time window.

UrgentVendorNum.description: If this field is not populated then the system will use the standard Supplier from PartPlant, or the last Supplier the part was purchased from, or the Supplier from the first Price list found for the part.

MMSExclude.description: Indicates if this part will be excluded in the Inventory Min/Max/Safety calculation.

MMSSales.description: Indicates if sales history for this part will be included in the Inventory Min/Max/Safety calculation.

MMSIssue.description: Indicates if job materials history for this part will be included in the Inventory Min/Max/Safety calculation.

MMSHistory.description: User defined number in days, of how far back to look in usage history.

MMSSafetyFactor.description: User defined, percentage of MIN to be set as Safety stock value.

MMSMaxFactor.description: User defined, used in calculation to defined MAX stock value.

SavedMinimumQty.description: WIll hold the proposed Min when the Min/Max/Safety process is ran

SavedMaximumQty.description: WIll hold the proposed Max when the Min/Max/Safety process is ran

ForecastDaysAfter.description: Number of days after the forecast date in which any sales orders that exist should reduce the forecast quantity. Ex: Forecast date of 3/31/98, Days after of 10, then any orders that have a date of 4/01/98 to 4/10/98 would reduce the forecast.
