EnableSerialNum.description: Used to indicate if the Serial Number format button should be enabled.

OnHandQty.description: A summary of PartBin.OnHandQty for the warehouses where the bin is a nettable bin (WhseBin.NonNettable = NO).

SNLeadingZeros.description: Used to designate the number of leading zeros for an Integer or Mask type serial number format.

HasOnHandQty.description: Indicates if there is any quantity on hand for this part

PartNum.description: The PartNum field identifies the Part and is used in the primary key.

Plant.description: Site Identifier. This field cannot be blank

PlanningAttributeSetSeq.description: The unique identifier of the Dynamic Attribute Planning Set.

PlanningAttributeSetHash.description: Hash key of the Company, AttrClassID, Planning Attributes and Planning Attribute Values.

MinimumQty.description: Indicates the desired minimum on-hand quantity. This is used by the time phase requirements report when user requests to show any parts that will or have fallen below this level. It is also used as a selection parameter for the inventory reorder report. This is an optional field.

MaximumQty.description: Use to set a Maximum quantity limit that is desired to be on-hand. This field is used as a selection option by the inventory reorder report to show all parts that are over this limit. This field is optional.

SafetyQty.description: Safety quantity is a "purchasing cushion" limit. It's the amount you would need to have to cover your requirements until a shipment arrives from the vendor. If your on-hand quantity falls below this limit it means that there is a good chance that you will run out of material before the next shipment arrives. This value is used by the inventory reorder report and the time phase report. It is an optional field.

MinOrderQty.description: Used to establish a suggested Order Qty when purchasing this Part for this Site. This value will be shown on the time phase report.

LeadTime.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

VendorNum.description: Number of Vendor master that this part is normally purchased from. The Purchase Order Management module uses it.  used in suggested vendor analysis.

MinMfgLotSize.description: This is the minimum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is less than this amount then MRP will create a job with this production quantity.  The excess amount will be sent to stock.  Zero is no minimum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.

MaxMfgLotSize.description: This is the maximum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is greater than this amount then MRP will create additional job(s) to satisfy the required production quantity.  Zero is no maximum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.  Example:  Required Quantity = 500, Maximum Lot Size = 150, 4 jobs will be created with production quantities of 150, 150, 150, and 50.

MfgLotMultiple.description: This is the manufacturing lot size multiple.  MRP will create jobs in multiples of this field.  Any excess amount will be sent to stock.  Zero is no lot multiple (lot-for-lot).  Example:  Required Quantity = 500, Lot Multiple = 150, Lot Maximum = 450, 2 jobs will be created with production quantities of 450, and 150.

DaysOfSupply.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

ReOrderLevel.description: This is the flag indicating the inventory level we need to bring up to when it falls below re-order point(safety + minimum). Valid values are MAX and MIN.

ForecastTime.description: Number of days forward to capture SugPODtl records for transfer as Forecast records.  Only for Intercompany trading partners.

TransferPlant.description: Default Site that part is transfered from when it is obtained via Site transfer.

TransferLeadTime.description: Used to record the normal order lead time for a Part from the transfer Site to this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional.

ShortHorizonDays.description: Number of days out that the ShortHorizonMinMfgLotSize and ShortHorizonMaxMfgLotSize will be used instead of MinLotSize and MaxLotSize.

ShortHorizonMinMfgLotSize.description: This is the Short Horizon minimum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is less than this amount then MRP will create a job with this production quantity.  The excess amount will be sent to stock.  Zero is no minimum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.

ShortHorizonMaxMfgLotSize.description: This is the Short Horizon maximum manufacturing lot size.  If the required quantity is greater than this amount then MRP will create additional job(s) to satisfy the required production quantity.  Zero is no maximum.  If nonzero, this field must be an even multiple of the MfgLotMultiple field.  Example:  Required Quantity = 500, Maximum Lot Size = 150, 4 jobs will be created with production quantities of 150, 150, 150, and 50.

ShortHorizonDaysSupp.description: Used to record the short horizon order lead time for a Part for this Site. This value is represented in days. It is optional. Used in calculation of suggested order dates, as a default value in job material detail records.

AutoConsumeStock.description: Auto consume available stock when MRP runs and creates a job.

StartMinLotQty.description: Start the minimum lot quantity for a job when there is enough quantity to do so.  If enough quantity is available for the minimum lot quantity, the job will be split - one job for the quantity that can be started, another job for the remaining quantity.  Used when MRP creates jobs.

MinLotLeadTime.description: The lead time to consider for constrained materials when determining if a quantity can be started on a job.  Applicable when StartMinLotQty is true.

MinStartQty.description: Indicates the minumum quantity that can be started when splitting a job.  Used when the StartMinLotQty option is selected.

UrgentLeadTime.description: This is the lead time used when generating a new suggestion within the lead time window.  If this field is 0 and the Supplier is determined from the Supplier Price List, the suggestion will use the lead time from the price list.

UrgentMinOrdQty.description: This is the minimum qty required when generating a new suggestion within the lead time window.

UrgentMultQty.description: This is used to calculate the suggestion qty to the nearest multiple when generating a new suggestion within the lead time window.

UrgentVendorNum.description: If this field is not populated then the system will use the standard Supplier from PartPlant, or the last Supplier the part was purchased from, or the Supplier from the first Price list found for the part.

SourceType.description: Indicates the normal source for this part in the Site.  Values are: M = Manufactured, P = Purchased, T = Transferred.  Initial default is base on Part.TypeCode.

ForecastDaysAfter.description: Number of days after the forecast date in which any sales orders that exist should reduce the forecast quantity.  Ex: Forecast date of 3/31/98, Days after of 10, then any orders that have a date of 4/01/98 to 4/10/98 would reduce the forecast.

Non compliant (Red) (one or more substances are selected but roll-up has not been executed or roll-up has failed) 3. Compliant (Green) (one or more substances are selected and roll-up was successful) 4. Exempt (Yellow) (when all substances are exempt ? verify exempt date)

AutoRecOpr.description:
The operation number PartOpr.OprSeq) that is marked to do the Automatic Receipt to inventory.  Note: Zero = no operation is set to perform the auto receive into inventory function.
This field is not directly maintainable. Instead it is set during operation maintenance by having the user mark a check box indicating "Auto Receive" which then updates this field with the operations sequence number.

FinalOpr.description:
The sequence of the operation that is to be considered as the operation which indicates the quantity complete for the Job/Assembly.  In other words this operations completed quantity = Job/Assembly completed.   Note: Zero = no operation is set and that the system should use the last operation to determine quantity complete.
This field is not directly maintainable. Instead it is set during operation maintenance by having the user mark a check box indicating "Final Operation" which then updates this field with the operations sequence number.

ConvFactor.description:
Value used to convert to/from base uom for the specific part. Cannot change if it's base uom of UOMClass (always 1.0) Sometimes a unit of measure conversion is relative to a specific part. These are non-standard conversions.
For example; 1 box(bx) of  Part A is 12ea. 1(bx) of Part B is 24ea. Non-standard conversions are defined in the PartUOM table. When ConvOperator = * then ConvFactor is 1 UOM expressed in Base UOM else it is 1 Base UOM expressed in UOM

Active.description: Indicates if the PartUOM is Active. This field is ready only and is used for internal logic purposes only.

TrackOnHand.description: If true then Onhand Quantity will be tracked per this UOM for this part.

Must be a UOMConv of the UOMClass with ClassType of "volume".   Use UOMClass.DefUOMCode of the "volume" UOMClass as a default  when creating new part records.
Having a Net Volume UOM will provides the ability to calculate total volume

HasBeenUsed.description: This indicates that this Part UOM has been used somewhere.  Therefore we do not want to allow the associated part specific conversion to change.

ConvOperator.description:
Indicates the mathmatical operator that is used for performing the UOM Conversion. Options are "*" (Multiply) or "\" (Divide).
For example for Feet to Inch where Inch is the base uom. 
You could have 1ft / 12 = 1In

ConvToUOM.description: Qualifies UOM of the ConvFactor. This either the UOMClass.BaseUOM or the UOMConv.UOMCode depending on the value of ConvOperator.

DispPkgDisplaySeq.description: Indicates the display sequence of the packaging in relation to the other packaging for the part.

DispPkgIsDefault.description: Indicates if the PkgCode is the default.

PartSpecificPkgCode.description: Indicates if the pkg code information seen in PartUOM is part specific (the data came from PackingUOM where PartNum is not blank.) If true the package code data can be updated in Part Maint, if false the PartUOM pkgcode columns will be disabled.

DispPkgDisplayHidden.description: Indicates if the package code will be displayed in the application. If false, the package codes that are not valid for the MGF process on the shop floor are hidden.

PartNum.description: Part Number

DemandQty.description:
A summary of the outstanding quantities for order open sales releases that are being filled from stock and of the open job material requirements that are to be issued from stock (JobMtl.Buyit = No) for this Part within a specific warehouse.
The system calculates Available as OnhandQty - AllocatedQty.

ReservedQty.description: A summary of the reserved quantities for order open sales releases for this Part within a specific warehouse. NOTE: This value is the TOTAL of reservations in PartAlloc.

AllocatedQty.description: A summary of the allocated quantities for order open sales releases for this Part within a specific warehouse. NOTE: This value is the TOTAL of allocation in PartAlloc.

PickingQty.description: Quantity that is in the picking process for orders. A summary of PartAlloc.PickingQty for all demand.

PickedQty.description: Stock Quantity picked for orders.  A summary of PartAlloc.PickedQty for all demand.

CountedDate.description: Date last counted.  Updated during the Inventory Posting Process.  Not directly maintainable by user.

OnHandQty.description: A summary of PartBin.OnHandQty for the warehouse where the bin is a nettable bin (WhseBin.NonNettable = NO). Maintained via the PartBin write trigger.

NonNettableQty.description: A summary of PartBin.OnHandQty for the warehouse where the bin is a non nettable bin (WhseBin.NonNettable = YES). Maintained via the PartBin write trigger.

BuyToOrderQty.description: A summary of Demand that is Buy To Order and thus should not take away from Available inventory.

SalesDemandQty.description:
A summary of the outstanding quantities for order open sales releases that are being filled from stock for this Part within a specific warehouse.
The system calculates Available as OnhandQty - AllocatedQty.

SalesReservedQty.description: A total of inventory quantities that has been reserved  for sales orders.   A summary of PartAlloc.ReservedQty for sales order allocations against stock  (PartAlloc.OrderNum > 0 & SupplyJobNum = blank).

SalesAllocatedQty.description: New in 9.00.  A summary of outstanding quantities for order open sales releases that are being filled from stock and have not been reserved, selected for picking or picked. Calculated as OurStockQty - ReservedQty + PickingQty + PickedQty).  Note: ReservedQty, PickingQty, PickedQty are summaries of PartAlloc records with a blank job,  related to an OrderRel.  The system tracks allocation summaries in the following sequence; AllocQty--> ReservedQty--> PickingQty--> PickedQty.

SalesPickingQty.description:
Quantity that is in the picking process for sales orders. A summary of PartAlloc.PickingQty where PartAlloc.OrderNum > 0
A summary of PartAlloc.PickingQty for sales order that are being picked from stock (PartAlloc.OrderNum > 0 & SupplyJobNum = blank).

SalesPickedQty.description: Stock Quantity picked for sales orders.  A summary of PartAlloc.PickedQty for sales order picked from stock  (PartAlloc.OrderNum > 0 & SupplyJobNum = blank).

JobDemandQty.description: A summary of the outstanding quantities for job material requirements that are being filled from stock for this Part within a specific warehouse.

JobReservedQty.description: Summary of mfg demands on released jobs. That is, a summary of outstanding JobMtl and JobAsmbl.PullQty where JobHead.JobFirm = Yes and JobReleased = Yes

JobAllocatedQty.description: New in 9.00.  Summary of stock allocated for jobs.

JobPickingQty.description: Quantity that is in the picking process for jobs. A summary of PartAlloc.PickingQty where PartAlloc.JobNum <> ''.

UnfirmJobDemandQty.description: New in 9.00.  Summary of mfg demand requirements on unfirm jobs. That is, a summary of outstanding JobMtl.RequiredQty and JobAsmbl.PullQty where JobHead.JobFirm = No.

TFOrdDemandQty.description:
A summary of the outstanding quantities for transfer order requirements that are being filled from stock for this Part within a specific warehouse.
The system calculates Available as OnhandQty - AllocatedQty.

TFOrdReservedQty.description: Summary of Transfer Order Reserved Qty for this Part in this Warehouse.

TFOrdAllocatedQty.description: Summary of Transfer Order Allocated Qty for this Part in this Warehouse.

TFOrdPickingQty.description: Quantity that is in the picking process for transfer orders.  A summary of PartAlloc.PickingQty where PartAlloc.TFOrdNum > 0.

TFOrdPickedQty.description: Stock Quantity picked for transfer orders.

MinimumQty.description: Indicates the desired minimum on-hand quantity. This is used by the time phase requirements report when user requests to show any parts that will or have fallen below this level. It is also used as a selection parameter for the inventory reorder report. This is an optional field.

MaximumQty.description: Use to set a Maximum quantity limit that is desired to be on-hand. This field is used as a selection option by the inventory reorder report to show all parts that are over this limit. This field is optional.

SafetyQty.description:
Safety quantity is a "purchasing cushion" limit. It's the amount you would need to have to cover your requirements until a shipment arrives from the vendor. If your on-hand quantity falls below this limit it means that there is a good chance that you will run out of material before the next shipment arrives. This value is used by the inventory reorder report and the time phase report. It is an optional field.
Note: Safety + Minimum = Reorder Point...  using this formula the  reorder point is the amount at which to reorder to maintain at least the prescribed minimum quantity.

KBPONUM.description: Purchase order number  that the detail line item is linked to.

KBPOLine.description: The line number of the detail record on the purchase order.  This number uniquely identifies the record within the Purchase Order number.  The number not directly maintainable, it's assigned by the system when records are created. The user references this item during PO receipt process.

KBBinNum.description: Identifies the Bin location that contains an Onhand quantity for this Part within a warehouse. The PartBin.BinNum can be blank (indicating bin tracking is not used for this part) or it  must be valid in the WhseBin table.

KBPlant.description: Site Identifier. This field cannot be blank.

KBQty.description: Indicates the desired minimum on-hand Kanban quantity.

PcntTolerance.description: Percent Tolerance. Entered as a positive number, Used to define the default percent tolerance. Zero indicates that any quantity variance is considered out of tolerance. A number greater than zero indicates that a percentage difference between the count quantity and the frozen quantity by more than the percent tolerance figure is considered out of tolerance. Calculated as ?adjustment qty / frozen qty? expressed as a percent.

CalcPcnt.description: False = there is no percent tolerance consideration and any percent variance is considered within tolerance. True = percent tolerance is active for this part/whs and the value in PcntTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance is within tolerance.

CalcQty.description: False = there is no quantity tolerance consideration and any quantity variance is considered within tolerance. True = quantity tolerance is active for this part/whse and the value in QtyTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance is within tolerance.

CalcValue.description: False = there is no value tolerance consideration and any value variance is considered within tolerance. True = value tolerance is active for this part/whse and the value in ValueTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance is within tolerance.

QtyAdjTolerance.description: Used to provide a means to control whether a count quantity discrepancy should be posted as an adjustment to inventory. Zero indicates all quantity adjustments will be posted. This parameter is used to control the count discrepancy of parts that are counted by weight on a scale. Counts often vary based upon humidity. If the count of the part is within this tolerance but different from the frozen quantity then no inventory adjustment will be posted.

CalcQtyAdj.description: False = there is no qty adj tolerance setting at the PartWhse level (so the setting in PartSite will be used). True = qty adj tolerance is active for this part/whs and the value in QtyAdjTolerance will be used to determine if the count variance should be

MinAbc.description: Moved from PartSite. When the system sets the ABC class, it will not allow the class to be lower than this class.

SystemAbc.description: Moved from PartSite. ABC Classification calculated by the system based on Stock Valuation Percent. This is maintainable by the user if ManualABC = true.

ManualABC.description: This will lock the SystemABC code preventing the automatic determination by the Calculate ABC Codes process, which will not reset the ABC code on parts that have the flag set.

LastCCDate.description: The last date this part was cycle counted. This date is used to insure that a part will be selected for cycle counting at the appropriate time. This is different than the CountedDate field, which will be updated for either a cycle count or physical inventory.

OvrrideCountFreq.description: This flag indicates whether the CountFreq defined in this record should over ride the count frequency in the AbcCode, SiteConfABC or WarehseABC tables.

CountFreq.description: If greater than zero this setting overrides values in AbcCode,  SiteConfABC and Warehse.

QtyTolerance.description: Quantity tolerance. Entered as a positive whole number, Used to define the default quantity tolerance. Zero indicates that any quantity variance is considered out of tolerance. A number greater than zero indicates that a count quantity that varies from the frozen quantity by more than the quantity tolerance figure is considered out of tolerance.

ValueTolerance.description: Value tolerance. Entered as a positive number. Used to define the default value tolerance.  An entry of zero will indicate that any value variance will be considered out of tolerance for this part. Value variance is calculated as the difference in cost between the counted quantity and the frozen quantity.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

BlanketPONum.description: Blanket PO to be used by default when buying a PO Suggestion.

BlanketPOLine.description: Blanket PO Line to be used by default when buying a PO Suggestion.

Plant.description: Filled in by BO, not phiscally in database.

IUM.description: Part Warehouse columns Unit Of Measure.

RelatedToFile.description:
Identifies the master file to which the GL Control is related to.  This field is used to properly isolate controls to the masters they are related to.
For example; Customer, PartClass identifies controls that are related to Customers and Part Classes

Key1.description: Major component of the foreign key of the related master record. For example: For a "Part"  control this field would contain the related Part Number,  for a "Customer"  it contains the Customer.CustNum.

Key2.description:
2nd component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent on the type of record.

TaxCode.description: Descriptive code assigned by user which uniquely identifies a Sales Tax master record.  Can't be blank. This is used as a foreign key in other files and may be used in displays/reports where space for the full description is limited.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PartNum.description: The PartNum field identifies the Part.
